phase1.explanation:
This is your first JavaFX window!

Application class:
• Extend Application and override start()
• launch() starts the application

start(Stage primaryStage):
• Called after launch()
• Contains all UI setup code

Stage:
• The main window
• setTitle() — sets window title
• setScene() — sets the content
• show() — makes window visible

Scene:
• Container for all UI nodes
• Takes root node and size

StackPane:
• Layout that stacks nodes on top of each other
• Centers children by default

Node:
• Any UI element (Label, Button, etc.)
• root.getChildren().add() adds nodes

Key steps:
1. Create UI nodes (Label, Button, etc.)
2. Create layout (StackPane, VBox, etc.)
3. Add nodes to layout
4. Create Scene with layout
5. Set Scene on Stage
6. Show Stage

⚠️ Always run JavaFX on the JavaFX Application Thread

phase2.errorMessage:
Test.java:9: error: cannot find symbol
    public void start(Stage primaryStage) {
                     ^
  symbol:   class Stage
  location: class Test
1 error
phase2.explanation:
You forgot to import javafx.stage.Stage!

FIX: Add import statement:
import javafx.stage.Stage;

Or use the fully qualified name:
javafx.stage.Stage primaryStage

Always check your imports when using JavaFX classes!

phase3.prompt:
Write a JavaFX program that:
1. Creates a Stage with title "My JavaFX App"
2. Creates a Label with text "Welcome to JavaFX!"
3. Uses a StackPane as the root layout
4. Creates a Scene with size 500x400
5. Shows the window

Inherit from Application and use launch() in main.
dialogueHints: Create StackPane root, add Label to it
phase3.openingDialogue: Let's create your first JavaFX window! 🪟
